Business Requirements Document (BRD)
Project: SmartInventory Pro — Warehouse Management System
Version: 1.0
Date: 2024-01-15
Author: Product Management Team

1. EXECUTIVE SUMMARY

SmartInventory Pro is a cloud-based warehouse management system designed to replace the legacy spreadsheet-driven inventory process at Meridian Logistics. The system will provide real-time stock visibility, automated reorder triggers, and multi-warehouse coordination for a network of up to 50 distribution centers. The primary goal is to reduce stockout incidents by 40% and cut manual data entry time by 60% within the first six months of deployment.

2. BUSINESS CONTEXT

Meridian Logistics currently manages inventory across 12 warehouses using a combination of Excel spreadsheets and a 15-year-old on-premise ERP system. The existing process requires warehouse staff to manually update stock counts twice daily, leading to data lag of up to 8 hours. This delay causes frequent stockouts on high-velocity SKUs and excess inventory on slow-moving items, resulting in an estimated $2.3M in annual losses.

3. OBJECTIVES

3.1 Primary Objectives
- Provide real-time inventory visibility across all warehouse locations
- Automate purchase order generation when stock falls below configurable reorder points
- Enable barcode and RFID scanning for receiving and dispatch operations
- Support multi-user concurrent access with role-based permissions

3.2 Secondary Objectives
- Generate daily and weekly inventory reports in PDF and CSV formats
- Integrate with existing ERP system via REST API
- Provide mobile-friendly interface for warehouse floor staff
- Maintain full audit trail of all inventory movements

4. FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS

4.1 Inventory Management
The system shall maintain a master catalog of all SKUs with attributes including product name, description, unit of measure, weight, dimensions, and supplier information. Stock levels shall be updated in real time upon scanning events. The system shall support lot tracking and expiry date management for perishable goods.

4.2 Reorder Automation
Users shall be able to configure minimum stock thresholds and reorder quantities per SKU per warehouse. When stock falls below the minimum threshold, the system shall automatically generate a draft purchase order and notify the procurement team via email. Procurement staff shall be able to approve, modify, or reject draft orders through the web interface.

4.3 User Management
The system shall support three user roles: Administrator, Warehouse Manager, and Warehouse Operator. Administrators can manage users and system configuration. Managers can approve orders and view all reports. Operators can perform scan operations and view their assigned warehouse data only.

4.4 Reporting
The system shall provide pre-built reports for daily stock levels, inventory turnover rate, stockout frequency, and supplier lead times. Reports shall be exportable in PDF and CSV formats. Custom date range selection shall be available for all reports.

5. NON-FUNCTIONAL REQUIREMENTS

5.1 Performance
The system shall support up to 500 concurrent users without degradation. Page load times shall not exceed 2 seconds under normal load. Barcode scan processing shall complete within 500 milliseconds.

5.2 Availability
The system shall maintain 99.9% uptime during business hours (06:00–22:00 local time). Scheduled maintenance windows shall not exceed 2 hours per month and must occur outside business hours.

5.3 Security
All data shall be encrypted in transit using TLS 1.2 or higher. User passwords shall be hashed using bcrypt. Session tokens shall expire after 8 hours of inactivity. All user actions shall be logged to an immutable audit trail.

6. CONSTRAINTS AND ASSUMPTIONS

The system must integrate with the existing SAP ERP instance via its published REST API. The mobile interface must support iOS 15+ and Android 11+. The initial deployment will cover 3 pilot warehouses before full rollout. Budget for the initial phase is capped at $450,000.

7. SUCCESS CRITERIA

The project will be considered successful when: stockout incidents decrease by at least 40% compared to the 6-month baseline, manual data entry time is reduced by at least 60%, and system uptime meets the 99.9% SLA for three consecutive months post-launch.
